texto: A book a week I heave a sigh;That Slogan's peremptory cryI will not hear, I will not heed.How can They say that I should needThe book They bid me weekly buy?But Slogans change, as days go by;My Psyche listens, fluttering shy,To newer message "Come and ReadA book a week."To read! to read! O wings that flyO'er sun-kissed lands, through clouded skyThat bear us on where Great ones lead!I too must follow, so I pleadFor magic wings. I'll read (or try)A book a week!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Alexander Ireland que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “A book a week I heave a sigh;That Slogan's peremptory cryI will not hear, I will not heed.How can…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, books, reading, com foco em mudanca e reading.
